steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The goal is to contrast the subject matter of the two prints. The notes describe Clarke's Hyde Park as "a tranquil, everyday scene" and Ndlovu's Amanzi Amthatha as featuring "an explicitly political point of view." The correct choice must use that subject-matter difference specifically, not dates or medium distinctions.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "The print by Ndlovu is a black-and-white linocut; by contrast, Clarke's is a relief print.": The notes clarify that linocutting is a type of relief printing, so calling one a linocut and the other a relief print does not describe a real difference. This also addresses medium, not subject matter. ✗
  • "Ndlovu's print expresses an explicitly political point of view, while Clarke's depicts a tranquil, everyday scene.": Directly contrasts the subject matter of the two prints using the exact language from the notes. ✓
  • "Clarke made *Hyde Park* in 1931, while Ndlovu made *Amanzi Amthatha* ("The cold water takes him") later, in 2001.": Contrasts the dates of creation, not the subject matter. ✗
  • "The scenes depicted in both works were first carved onto a printing block, then stamped onto paper; however, one work is a linocut, while the other is a relief print.": Focuses on printing technique and again falsely distinguishes linocut from relief print. Does not address subject matter. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
